candidates.title: The Clean Thought That Lands
candidates.hook: A strong linear reel already opens with a question and resolves with a clean answer.
candidates.summary: A contiguous candidate with a clear beginning, middle, and ending.
candidates.start_anchor_quote: The question starts cleanly for viewers
candidates.end_anchor_quote: the ending resolves the idea clearly
candidates.subtitle_intro: The question starts cleanly for viewers
candidates.social_caption: A reel works best when the hook, context, and payoff already live together.
candidates.why_it_works: This candidate stays linear because the source range already has a strong hook, understandable context, and clean ending.
candidates.display_teaser: A contiguous moment with its own beginning, middle, and payoff.
candidates.opening_hook: The question starts cleanly for viewers
candidates.closing_line: the ending resolves the idea clearly
candidates.edit_decision_rationale: Linear is preferred because the source range already has a strong beginning, clear middle, and satisfying ending.
candidates.core_question: When should a reel stay contiguous?
candidates.conflict: Reordering would add complexity without improving clarity.
candidates.payoff: The thought lands cleanly without needing a constructed timeline.
candidates.rationale: Contiguous source range is the cleanest edit.
captions: The question starts cleanly for viewers.

candidates.title: Move the Hook First
candidates.hook: The strongest line appears later, so the edit opens with that hook before returning to context.
candidates.summary: A constructed story edit that moves a later hook to the front, then returns to earlier context and ends on a closing button.
candidates.start_anchor_quote: The later line becomes the cold open
candidates.end_anchor_quote: the closing button pays it off
candidates.subtitle_intro: The later line becomes the cold open
candidates.social_caption: Sometimes the best reel starts with the line that originally came later.
candidates.why_it_works: The reordered hook creates immediate curiosity, the earlier context explains it, and the closing line resolves the idea without changing meaning.

timeline_segments.reason_for_placement: This later line is the strongest curiosity hook, so it becomes the cold open.
Earlier context explains the hook without forcing viewers through a weaker opening.
The closing button lands immediately after the viewer understands the context.

candidates.display_teaser: A later line becomes the opener, then the edit returns to context.
candidates.edit_decision_rationale: The source opening is awkward, but the later hook honestly previews the same idea and is paid off by the following context.
candidates.core_question: What if the strongest hook comes after the context?
candidates.conflict: The original beginning is weaker than the line that appears later.
candidates.payoff: The reordered edit gives the viewer the strongest line first and then explains it.
candidates.rationale: Hook-reordered recipe is stronger than the original contiguous opening while preserving meaning.
captions: The later line becomes the cold open.

candidates.title: Setup, Escalate, Pay Off
candidates.hook: A short hook introduces the question, context raises stakes, and a final line lands the lesson.
candidates.summary: A three-part story edit that uses a hook, setup/context, and payoff from real source utterances.
candidates.start_anchor_quote: The short hook asks the question clearly
candidates.end_anchor_quote: the payoff reframes the whole moment
candidates.subtitle_intro: The short hook asks the question clearly
candidates.social_caption: The best version is hook, context, then payoff — not just a quote.
candidates.why_it_works: The edit creates a clean mini-story while keeping each segment tied to real utterances and source timecodes.

timeline_segments.reason_for_placement: The question is a clean cold open and establishes the viewer's curiosity.
The setup supplies enough context for the answer to make sense.
The payoff ends the thought cleanly after the context lands.

candidates.display_teaser: Question, context, and payoff assembled into a coherent short reel.
candidates.edit_decision_rationale: The story edit uses adjacent source material and only reorganizes emphasis, not meaning.
candidates.core_question: How do you turn one idea into a satisfying reel?
candidates.conflict: A quote alone lacks the setup needed to feel complete.
candidates.payoff: The payoff reframes the moment as a complete mini-story.
candidates.rationale: Hook/setup/payoff recipe is coherent and easy for the app to validate.
captions: The short hook asks the question clearly.

candidates.title: Too Much Context Required
candidates.hook: This low-confidence example shows how a candidate can be represented without being considered ready.
candidates.summary: A rejected/review example with high context dependency and missing payoff risk.
candidates.start_anchor_quote: The idea starts but needs more context
candidates.end_anchor_quote: the ending does not fully pay off
candidates.subtitle_intro: The idea starts but needs more context
candidates.social_caption: Not every plausible moment should become a reel.
candidates.why_it_works: This is intentionally low confidence: it demonstrates review flags without exposing private transcript text.
candidates.display_teaser: A low-confidence example that should be reviewed or rejected.
candidates.opening_hook: The idea starts but needs more context
candidates.closing_line: the ending does not fully pay off
candidates.edit_decision_rationale: The candidate remains linear but should not be promoted because it lacks standalone payoff.
candidates.conflict: The premise depends on prior conversation.
candidates.payoff: The answer is incomplete and should be rejected or reviewed.
candidates.rationale: Included as a review/rejection contract example, not a recommended reel.
captions: The idea starts but needs more context.
candidates.safety_notes: Review example only; high context dependency.

model_notes: Backend-generated mock/golden Smart Story Edit fixture. No live OpenAI call was made. Transcript excerpts are redacted placeholders.
